subject: Ecology
title: Blue Carbon
description: A quick win: understand Blue Carbon.

hook.text: Coastal wetlands store carbon in their sediments for thousands of years—this is blue carbon.

buildup.text: Mangroves, salt marshes, and seagrasses lock carbon in waterlogged mud where it can't decompose.

discovery.text: Blue carbon ecosystems hold five times more carbon per hectare than most terrestrial forests.

twist.text: When these ecosystems are destroyed, centuries of stored carbon is released rapidly.

climax.text: Blue carbon credits are emerging as a financial tool to fund coastal wetland protection.

punchline.text: The ocean's carbon vault is hidden in coastal mud.

quiz.question: What is blue carbon?
options: Carbon stored by coastal wetland ecosystems
Carbon dissolved in deep ocean water
